title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. pseudocaudatum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. pseudocaudatum, also known as the bristle fern or Japanese bristle fern, is a subspecies of the bracken fern (Pteridium aquilinum) native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant effects. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. pseudocaudatum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or extracts, although it is essential to note that the plant's toxicity and potential carcinogenic effects should be carefully considered before use.
